topic: Lindy AI Review 2026: No-Code AI Agent Automation Platform

key_points: Positions Lindy as a top AI agent builder for non-technical users
Highlights 4,000+ integrations and no-code interface
Reviews credit system and cost implications in detail
Recommends for teams wanting AI-first automation over rule-based tools
Focuses on no-code automation without any coding knowledge
Covers Gaia voice agent and Autopilot virtual computer features
Highlights 5,000+ integrations as of March 2026
Compares Lindy favorably to Zapier for AI-driven tasks
Frames Lindy as an 'AI Employee' rather than just automation tool
Discusses credit limits and cost unpredictability as top concerns
Reviews Lindy Build for creating web apps with no code
Emphasizes SOC 2 and HIPAA compliance for enterprise use
3-month hands-on review with real workflow testing results
Details credit burn rate on common sales and support workflows
Highlights AI email parsing and lead enrichment use cases
Recommends Lindy for SMBs but flags billing surprises
Comprehensive feature-by-feature walkthrough of Lindy platform
Tests 1,000+ templates across sales, support, and operations
Covers Gaia voice agent pricing ($0.19/minute with GPT-4o)
Rates ease of use as Lindy's strongest differentiator vs competitors

stats.fact: Lindy AI has a G2 rating of 4.9/5 based on 170 verified user reviews
Lindy supports 5,000+ integrations as of March 2026, up from 4,000+ in 2025
Lindy offers 1,000+ pre-made agent templates for common business workflows
Lindy serves 5,000+ customers across various industries
Lindy has raised $54M in total funding, including a $35M Series B in January 2023
Lindy hit $5.1M in annual revenue as of October 2024 with a 37-person team
Gaia voice agent beats competitors by more than 500ms in response latency, powered by Deepgram Flux
Lindy integrated Claude Sonnet 4.5 in September 2025, achieving 77.2% on SWE-bench Verified and 30+ hours autonomous operation
Ease of use mentioned in 125 of 170 G2 reviews, the most-cited feature
Free plan provides 400 credits/month; basic automations use 1 credit, AI-intensive tasks use 5-10 credits each
Make offers 10,000 operations at $9/month vs Zapier's 750 tasks at $19.99 — 13x better value for high-volume automation
Lindy Autopilot gives AI agents access to a virtual computer in the cloud, enabling web browsing, spreadsheet updates, and cross-app actions without API limits

angles: The 'AI Employee' framing: why Lindy positions itself beyond traditional no-code automation and what that means for teams replacing manual work
Credit system gotchas: real-world credit burn rates on common workflows vs. the advertised free plan — what most reviews don't tell you
Gaia voice agent deep dive: how Lindy's sub-500ms phone AI competes with dedicated voice platforms like Vapi and Bland AI
Autopilot (virtual computer): Lindy's most underrated feature that lets agents operate like a real employee with screen access
Lindy Build: creating functional web apps and internal dashboards with AI — the feature that makes Lindy more than a workflow tool
Lindy vs. Zapier vs. Make vs. n8n: a decision framework for teams choosing between AI-first and rule-based automation
Compliance advantage: SOC 2 and HIPAA certification making Lindy viable for healthcare and finance teams where other no-code tools fail
When NOT to use Lindy: cases where Make or n8n outperform it on cost efficiency and data handling precision

H2: Gaia Voice Agent and Autopilot: Lindy's Power Features Reviewed
H2: Lindy AI Weaknesses: Where It Falls Short

H2: Final Verdict: Is Lindy AI Worth the Price?
